service_name: Entry Visa for Residents of GCC Countries and Their Companions
quick_summary: Allows GCC residents and their companions to enter the UAE for a short visit. The visa is valid for 30 days and can be extended once for an additional 30 days.
documents_required: Passport or travel document (valid for at least 6 months)
Recent passport-size photo with white background
eligibility_requirements: Must be a resident of a GCC country
GCC residence permit must be valid
Profession must be among the approved professions
Applicant must not have any entry restrictions to the UAE
service_steps: Log in to https://smart.gdrfad.gov.ae
Register as a user
Choose the required service
Fill in the application details
Pay the required fees
After approval, the visa is sent to the user's email
extension: Can be extended once for an additional 30 days
important_notes: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months
GCC residence permit must be valid
Visa must be obtained before travelling
Profession is subject to approval

quick_summary: Allows foreigners to enter the UAE to search for employment without requiring a sponsor.
documents_required: Passport copy (valid for at least 6 months)
Bachelor's degree or equivalent qualification certificate
eligibility_requirements: Hold a Bachelor's degree or equivalent qualification
Be a skilled professional in Skill Level 1, 2, or 3 according to MOHRE OR be a graduate from a top 500 university within the last 2 years
Meet financial guarantee requirements
service_steps: Option 1: Apply through digital channels (website or smart application).
Log in to the smart services system using UAE Pass or username.
Search for the required service.
Fill in the application data.
Upload the required documents if requested.
Pay the service fee (if applicable).
Submit the application and wait for approval.
Option 2: Apply through a Customer Happiness Centre.
Visit the nearest Customer Happiness Centre.
Take an automated queue ticket and wait for your turn.
Submit the application and required documents to the customer service employee.
Pay the service fee (if applicable).
Option 3: Apply through an Amer Service Centre.
Visit the nearest Amer Service Centre.
Take an automated queue ticket and wait for your turn.
Submit the application and required documents to the customer service employee.
Pay the service fee (if applicable).
important_notes: No sponsor or host is required
Additional service fees may apply
Requirements may change, verify on the official website

service_name: Visit Visa for Exploring Business Opportunities
quick_summary: Allows foreign entrepreneurs and investors to visit the UAE to explore business and investment opportunities without requiring a sponsor.
documents_required: Passport copy (valid for at least 6 months)
Documents supporting business or investment activity (if applicable)
eligibility_requirements: Applicant must be an entrepreneur, investor, or business professional
Applicant must meet the financial requirements set by UAE authorities
Passport must be valid for at least 6 months
service_steps: Option 1: Apply through digital channels (GDRFA website or smart application).
Log in to the smart services system using UAE Pass or username.
Search for the required service.
Fill out the application information.
Pay the service fees (if applicable).
Option 2: Apply through a Customer Happiness Center.
Visit the nearest Customer Happiness Center.
Obtain an automated queue ticket.
Submit the application and required documents to the customer service employee.
Pay the service fees (if applicable).
Option 3: Apply through an Amer Service Center.
Visit the nearest Amer Service Center.
Obtain an automated queue ticket.
Submit the application and required documents to the customer service employee.
Pay the service fees (if applicable).
important_notes: No sponsor or host is required
Additional service fees may apply
Business-related supporting documents may be requested
Requirements may change, verify on the official website before applying

quick_summary: Allows foreign patients to enter the UAE for medical treatment under the sponsorship of a licensed healthcare facility.
documents_required: Passport copy (valid for at least 6 months)
Letter from the healthcare facility stating the reason for the visit
eligibility_requirements: Must require medical treatment in the UAE
Must be sponsored by a licensed healthcare facility in the UAE
Must have valid health insurance
Must provide the required medical documents
service_steps: Apply through digital channels (GDRFA website or smart application).
Log in to the smart services system using UAE Pass or username.
Search for the required Medical Visa service.
Fill out the application information.
Upload the required documents.
Pay the service fees.
Submit the application and wait for approval.
important_notes: The sponsor must be a licensed hospital or healthcare facility in the UAE.
Medical insurance is mandatory.
A financial security deposit may be required.
The visa can be single-entry or multiple-entry.
A patient's companion may also apply for a companion medical visa.
Medical treatment visas may be extended according to UAE regulations.
Additional fees apply if the sponsored person is already inside the UAE.
The total visa cost may vary depending on the applicant's circumstances.

quick_summary: Allows employees working in the UAE private sector to legally reside and work in the UAE. The visa is typically valid for 2 years and can be renewed.
documents_required: Recent passport-size photo with white background
Medical fitness certificate (for applicants aged 18 and above)
eligibility_requirements: Must have a job offer or employment with a UAE private sector company
Employer must sponsor the residence visa
Must pass the required medical fitness test
service_steps: Apply through digital channels (GDRFA website or smart application).
Log in to the smart services system using UAE Pass or username.
Search for the Residence Visa - Private Sector service.
Fill in the application data.
Upload the required documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
Submit the application and wait for approval.
important_notes: The employer is responsible for applying for the residence visa.
Applicants aged 18 and above must complete a medical fitness test.
An Emirates ID application is required as part of the residence visa process.
The issuance fee increases by AED 100 annually whenever the residency period exceeds two years.
The grace period after residence visa expiry or cancellation is 60 days.
Visa validity and fees may change based on regulations.

quick_summary: Allows employees working in the UAE public sector to legally reside in the UAE. The visa is typically valid for 2 years and is renewable.
documents_required: Recent personal photo with white background
Medical examination report approved by relevant authorities (for applicants over 18 years old)
eligibility_requirements: Must be employed in the UAE public sector
Must pass the required medical examination
Passport must be valid for more than 6 months
service_steps: Log in to the smart services system using UAE Pass or username
Search for the Residence Visa - Public Sector service
Fill in the application data
Upload required documents
Pay the service fee (if applicable)
Submit the application and wait for approval
terms_and_conditions: Medical examination must not exceed three months validity
Identity Card application validity must not exceed 120 days
Passport must be valid for more than six months
important_notes: The issuance fee increases by AED 100 annually whenever residency exceeds two years
The grace period after expiry or cancellation is 60 days
All applications are subject to approval by relevant authorities

quick_summary: Allows UAE employers to legally hire a new employee under a valid work permit issued by MOHRE.
documents_required: Signed job offer from the employer
Additional documents as requested by MOHRE
eligibility_requirements: Establishment must have an available electronic quota
Employee must be 18 years or older
Establishment must have a valid trade license with no violations
Applicant must not already hold an active work permit
Application must be submitted by authorized signatory of the establishment
service_steps: Log in using username/password or UAE Pass (for online channels)
Submit the work permit application through approved service channels
Application is reviewed by MOHRE for compliance with requirements
Employer is notified to complete any missing documents if required
Once approved, the work permit is issued
Pay federal fees, bank guarantee or insurance (if applicable)

fees.note: MOHRE website and app services are free excluding taxes and collection charges

important_notes: Permit replacement and nationality change allowed under specific conditions within six months
The profession and gender must remain unchanged during replacement
Employer must ensure all legal requirements are met before applying
Fees do not include VAT or collection charges

quick_summary: Allows international students enrolled in an accredited UAE educational institution to reside in the UAE for the duration of their studies.
documents_required: Passport copy (valid for at least 6 months)
Recent passport-size photo with white background
University admission letter or study certificate
eligibility_requirements: Must be enrolled in an accredited educational institution in the UAE
Must be sponsored by the university or an eligible parent
Passport must be valid for at least 6 months
Must meet all UAE residency requirements
service_steps: Log in to the smart services system using UAE Pass or username.
Search for the Student Residence Visa service.
Fill in the application data.
Upload the required documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
Submit the application and wait for approval.
visa_validity: Valid for the duration of the study program
important_notes: The university usually manages the visa process for international students.
Students may be sponsored by a parent who is a UAE resident if eligible.
The student is allowed to remain in the UAE for up to 180 days after completing studies.
Passport must be valid for at least 6 months.

service_name: Golden Residence Permit - Top Students and Graduates
quick_summary: Allows outstanding high school students and top university graduates from UAE and international universities to obtain a 10-year Golden Residence Permit in the UAE.

top_students: High school certificate with minimum 95% score
Current UAE residence permit copy (Dubai-issued if applicable)
uae_university_graduates: Current UAE residence permit copy (Dubai-issued if applicable)
international_university_graduates: Current UAE residence permit copy (if available)

top_students: High school certificate from a school licensed by the Ministry of Education or relevant authority
Certificate must be attested by the Ministry of Education
uae_university_graduates: Bachelor's degree from a university licensed by the Ministry of Education
Certificate must be attested by the Ministry of Higher Education
international_university_graduates: University must be ranked among the world's top 100 universities approved by the Ministry of Education
Certificate must be attested by the Ministry of Higher Education

service_steps: Option 1: Apply through GDRFA digital channels.
Log in using UAE Pass or username.
Search for the Golden Residence Permit service.
Fill in the application details.
Upload the required documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
Submit the application and wait for approval.
Option 2: Apply through an Amer Service Center.
Visit the nearest Amer Service Center.
Take a queue ticket.
Submit the application and documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
important_notes: Applicant must obtain UAEICP approval before applying.
A Dubai-issued residency file is required for student Golden Residency applications.
Golden Residency applications require approval through UAEICP or nomination by a competent authority.
Golden Residency for students is not renewed after expiry.
Top high school students may sponsor their parents only.
University graduates may sponsor spouse and children if family sponsorship requirements are met.
Golden Residency holders are exempt from the 180-day residence rule.

service_name: Golden Residence Permit - Pioneers of Humanitarian Work
quick_summary: Allows individuals who have made significant contributions to humanitarian work inside or outside the UAE to obtain a long-term Golden Residence Permit.
documents_required: Recommendation from the Ministry of Community Empowerment
eligibility_requirements: Must obtain approval from the Ministry of Community Empowerment
Must be a member or specialized employee of an international or regional humanitarian organization for at least 5 years, OR
Must be a member or specialized employee of a public benefit association or institution for at least 5 years, OR
Must have received local, regional, or international humanitarian awards, OR
Must have provided humanitarian financial support of at least AED 2,000,000, OR
Must hold a university degree
service_steps: Obtain nomination or recommendation from the Ministry of Community Empowerment.
Receive approval through the UAEICP system.
Apply through the Golden Residency Office or approved service channels.
Log in using UAE Pass or username.
Search for the Golden Residence Permit service.
Fill in the application details.
Upload the required documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
Submit the application and wait for approval.
Alternative: Apply through an Amer Service Centre.
Visit the nearest Amer Service Centre.
Take a queue ticket.
Submit the application and required documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
important_notes: Approval from the Ministry of Community Empowerment is mandatory.
Nomination does not guarantee final approval.
Final approval is subject to GDRFA review.
Applicant must have a Dubai-issued residence file.
Golden Residence holders are exempt from the 180-day stay outside UAE rule.
Applicants must be able to support themselves and their family without government assistance.

service_name: Golden Residence Permit - Specialized Scientists and Professionals
quick_summary: Allows scientists, researchers, specialists, healthcare professionals, educators, industrial experts, and highly skilled workers to obtain a 10-year Golden Residence Permit in the UAE.
documents_required: Nomination letter from the relevant UAE authority
Employment contract or salary certificate (for skilled workers)
Bank statement showing salary transfers for the last 6 months
eligibility_requirements: Must belong to an approved specialized field
Must obtain nomination from the relevant UAE authority
Must meet educational qualification requirements
Skilled workers must earn at least AED 30,000 per month
Must have valid health insurance
Must satisfy UAEICP and GDRFA approval requirements
service_steps: Obtain nomination from the relevant authority.
Receive UAEICP approval.
Log in using UAE Pass or username.
Search for the Golden Residence Permit service.
Fill in the application data.
Upload required documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
Submit the application and wait for approval.
renewable: Yes (if eligibility requirements continue to be met)
important_notes: Final approval is subject to GDRFA review.
Golden Residence holders are exempt from the 180-day stay abroad rule.
The permit may be cancelled if eligibility conditions are no longer met.
Salary requirements must continue to be maintained for skilled workers.

quick_summary: Allows entrepreneurs with innovative or technology-focused business projects to obtain a 10-year Golden Residence Permit in the UAE.
documents_required: Nomination letter from Dubai Future Foundation
eligibility_requirements: Must be nominated by Dubai Future Foundation
Must own an innovative or future-focused business project
Project must be registered with the Ministry of Economy or relevant local authority
OR have a project generating at least AED 1 million annual revenue
OR have established a business generating at least AED 2 million revenue
OR have sold a startup or business for at least AED 7 million
service_steps: Obtain nomination from Dubai Future Foundation.
Log in to the smart services system using UAE Pass or username.
Search for the Golden Residence Permit - Entrepreneurs service.
Fill in the application details.
Upload the required documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
Submit the application and wait for approval.
Alternative: Apply through an Amer Service Center.
Visit the nearest Amer Service Center.
Take a queue ticket.
Submit the application and documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
renewable: Yes (if eligibility conditions continue to be met)
important_notes: Nomination from Dubai Future Foundation is required.
Nomination does not guarantee final approval.
Final approval is subject to GDRFA review.
Golden Residence holders are exempt from the 180-day stay outside UAE rule.
Applicants must be able to financially support themselves and their family.

quick_summary: Allows eligible investors to obtain a 10-year Golden Residence Permit through business investment, real estate investment, bank deposits, or significant tax contributions in the UAE.
documents_required: Supporting financial documents based on investor category
eligibility_requirements: Own a company investment worth at least AED 2 million
OR own real estate worth at least AED 2 million
OR hold a bank deposit of at least AED 2 million in a UAE bank
OR pay taxes of at least AED 250,000 annually in the UAE
service_steps: Log in using UAE Pass or username.
Search for the Golden Residence Permit - Investors service.
Fill in the application details.
Upload the required documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
Submit the application and wait for approval.
Alternative: Apply through an Amer Service Center.
Visit the nearest Amer Service Center.
Take a queue ticket.
Submit the application and documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
renewable: Yes (if eligibility requirements continue to be met)
important_notes: Investment must meet the minimum required value.
Real estate investors must maintain ownership during the Golden Visa period.
Bank deposit investors must keep the deposit frozen according to UAE regulations.
Final approval is subject to GDRFA review.
Golden Residence holders are exempt from the 180-day stay outside UAE rule.
Applicants must be able to financially support themselves and their family.

quick_summary: Allows individuals with exceptional talent in culture, arts, innovation, sports, or digital technology to obtain a 10-year Golden Residence Permit in the UAE.
documents_required: Nomination letter from the relevant UAE authority
Supporting documents showing achievements or talent
eligibility_requirements: Must demonstrate exceptional talent or achievements in an approved field
Must receive nomination or recommendation from the relevant UAE authority
Must meet the requirements of the nominated category
service_steps: Obtain nomination from the relevant authority.
Log in using UAE Pass or username.
Search for the Golden Residence Permit - Talented Individuals service.
Fill in the application details.
Upload required documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
Submit the application and wait for approval.
Alternative: Apply through an Amer Service Center.
Visit the nearest Amer Service Center.
Take a queue ticket.
Submit the application and documents.
Pay the applicable fees.
renewable: Yes (if eligibility conditions continue to be met)
important_notes: Nomination from the relevant UAE authority is required.
Nomination does not guarantee final approval.
Final approval is subject to GDRFA review.
Applicants must be able to financially support themselves and their family.
Golden Residence holders are exempt from the 180-day stay outside UAE rule.

quick_summary: Allows eligible residents and citizens to obtain a new UAE driving licence after completing training, passing required tests, and paying applicable fees.
eligibility_requirements: Must meet the minimum age requirement for the vehicle category
Must be medically fit
Must complete required driving training
Must pass knowledge, yard, and road tests
service_steps: Log in using Emirates ID, Passport, or UAE Pass
Select a driving institute
Submit the application
Pay initial fees
Receive learning permit
Complete driving training
Pass eye test
Pass knowledge, yard, and road tests
Pay licence issuance fees
Receive digital or physical driving licence
processing_time: Instant after passing tests and paying fees
important_notes: Eye test is valid for 3 years
People of Determination may receive service discounts
Training requirements depend on driving experience
A temporary licence is issued after passing the final road test
Applications may be suspended if no test is taken within 6 months

quick_summary: Allows UAE residents and citizens to renew an existing driving licence online after completing the required eye test and paying applicable fees.
eligibility_requirements: Must hold a valid or expired UAE driving licence
Must complete an eye test
Must pay all outstanding traffic fines
Medical fitness test required for professional drivers
service_steps: Complete an eye test at an approved centre
Log in using Emirates ID, Passport, or UAE Pass
Select Driving Licence Renewal service
Verify licence details
Pay fines and renewal fees
Choose licence delivery method
Receive digital or physical driving licence

special_case.expired_more_than_10_years: Applicant must pass an Evaluation Test before renewal

important_notes: Driving licence can be renewed 6 months before expiry if aged 21 or above
Driving licence can be renewed 3 months before expiry if under 21
Eye test is valid for 3 years
Medical test is valid for 6 months
People of Determination may receive service discounts
Licences expired for more than 10 years require an Evaluation Test
All outstanding traffic fines must be paid before renewal

authority: Ministry of Interior (MOI) / UAE Traffic Authorities
quick_summary: Allows eligible residents to exchange a valid foreign driving licence from approved countries for a UAE driving licence without completing full driving tests.
target_audience: UAE residents with valid foreign driving licence
documents_required: Valid Emirates ID or residence visa
eligibility_requirements: Must be at least 18 years old
Driving licence must be valid
Licence must be from an approved country for exchange
Applicant must have UAE residence visa or valid residence status
Must pass eye test
service_steps: Submit application through digital channels
Fill in personal and licence details
Complete eye test if required
Pay applicable fees
Submit original foreign driving licence (if required)
Receive UAE driving licence
important_notes: Only licences from approved countries can be exchanged
Original licence may need to be surrendered for some countries
Eye test is mandatory
Service availability depends on Ministry of Interior approval list

authority: Invest in Dubai / Department of Economy and Tourism (Dubai)
quick_summary: Allows entrepreneurs to legally establish a business in Dubai by issuing a trade licence for commercial, professional, or home-based activities.

licence_types.description: Standard business licence requiring Memorandum of Association and physical office lease.
Fast-track licence issued within minutes for selected activities without external approvals.
includes: Option to employ up to 3 employees

licence_types.description: Licence for home-based businesses and sole establishments in Dubai.

eligibility_requirements: Valid Emirates ID or unified number
Business activity must be approved under Dubai regulations
Some activities require external approvals
service_steps: Select licence type (Normal / Instant / eTrader)
Submit application online or via service centre
Provide required documents
Obtain approvals if needed
Pay licence fees
Receive trade licence digitally

processing_time.normal_licence: Up to 10 minutes via service centres (varies by approvals)

important_notes: Instant licence is only for activities that do not require external approvals
eTrader licence is limited to home-based businesses
Normal licence requires physical office lease
Additional approvals may apply depending on business activity

service_name: Visit Visa for Visiting Relatives and Friends
quick_summary: Allows residents or sponsors in the UAE to bring relatives or friends for a short visit. Visa validity depends on entry type and can be extended within UAE rules.
documents_required: Residence permit copy of sponsor (if applicable)
Copy of GCC residence permit (if applicable)
Copy of identification document (for Afghanistan / Iran / Iraq cases)
eligibility_requirements: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months
Valid health insurance in UAE is required
Sponsor must meet minimum salary requirement depending on relationship
No entry restrictions to UAE
Proof of relationship must be provided for relatives

visa_validity.maximum_extension: Up to 180 days total within one year

extension_policy: Visa may be extended once depending on approval
service_steps: Log in to UAE smart services portal or GDRFA system
Register or sign in using UAE Pass or username
Select 'Visit Visa for Relatives and Friends' service
Fill in sponsor and applicant details
Upload required documents
Verify relationship and eligibility criteria
Pay application fees and security deposit if required
Submit application for approval
Receive visa approval via email or system notification
Visa is issued and sent digitally
refund_policy: Financial guarantee may be refunded after visa cancellation or exit from UAE if no violations exist
important_notes: Health insurance is mandatory in UAE
Passport must be valid for at least 6 months
Overstay fine applies from the day after visa expiry
Visa extension depends on UAE authority approval
Proof of kinship is strictly required for relatives
Salary requirements differ based on relationship category
